heading: Degree granting authority for National Defense University
label: 2163 Degree granting authority for National Defense University
notes: Amendments2008— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section related to conferral of master of science and master of arts degrees by National Defense University.Pub. L. 110–417, substituted “National Defense University: master’s degree programs” for “National Defense University: master of science degrees” in section catchline.Pub. L. 110–181, § 526(b)(1)Subsec. (a). , inserted “or master of arts” after “master of science”.Pub. L. 110–181, § 526(a)(1)Subsec. (b)(4). , added par. (4).Pub. L. 110–181, § 526(a)(2)2006— amended section catchline and text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows:Pub. L. 109–163“(a) .—The President of the National Defense University, upon the recommendation of the faculty and commandant of the National War College, may confer the degree of master of science of national security strategy upon graduates of the National War College who fulfill the requirements for the degree.National War College Degree“(b) ICAF .—The President of the National Defense University, upon the recommendation of the faculty and commandant of the Industrial College of the Armed Forces, may confer the degree of master of science of national resource strategy upon graduates of the Industrial College of the Armed Forces who fulfill the requirements for the degree.Degree“(c) .—The authority provided by subsections (a) and (b) shall be exercised under regulations prescribed by the Secretary of Defense.”Regulations

Effective Date of 2008 Amendment, [div. A], title V, § 543(j), , , provided that:Pub. L. 110–417122 Stat. 4465Oct. 14, 2008“The amendments made by this section [amending this section and sections 2161, 4314, 4321, 7048, 7101, 7102, 9314, and 9317 of this title] shall apply to any degree granting authority established, modified, or redesignated on or after the date of enactment of this Act [] for an institution of professional military education referred to in such amendments.”Oct. 14, 2008, , , provided that:Pub. L. 110–181, div. A, title V, § 526(c)122 Stat. 105Jan. 28, 2008“Paragraph (4) of , United States Code, as added by subsection (a) of this section, applies with respect to any person who becomes a graduate of the National Defense University on or after , and fulfills the requirements of the program referred to in such paragraph (4).”section 2163(b) of title 10September 6, 2006

Effective Date of 2006 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 109–163, div. A, title V, § 521(c)119 Stat. 3240Jan. 6, 2006“Paragraph (3) of , United States Code, as amended by subsection (a), shall take effect for degrees awarded after May 2005.”section 2163(b) of title 10

children.heading: .—Authority
children.label: a .—Authority
children.text: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary of Defense, the President of the National Defense University may, upon the recommendation of the faculty of the National Defense University, confer appropriate degrees upon graduates who meet the degree requirements.
the Secretary of Education has recommended approval of the degree in accordance with the Federal Policy Governing Granting of Academic Degrees by Federal Agencies; and
the National Defense University is accredited by the appropriate civilian academic accrediting agency or organization to award the degree, as determined by the Secretary of Education.

children.label: A
children.text: a copy of the self assessment questionnaire required by the Federal Policy Governing Granting of Academic Degrees by Federal Agencies, at the time the assessment is submitted to the Department of Education’s National Advisory Committee on Institutional Quality and Integrity; and

children.label: B
children.text: the subsequent recommendations and rationale of the Secretary of Education regarding the establishment of the degree granting authority.

Upon any modification or redesignation of existing degree granting authority, the Secretary of Defense shall submit to the Committees on Armed Services of the Senate and House of Representatives a report containing the rationale for the proposed modification or redesignation and any subsequent recommendation of the Secretary of Education on the proposed modification or redesignation.

The Secretary of Defense shall submit to the Committees on Armed Services of the Senate and House of Representatives a report containing an explanation of any action by the appropriate academic accrediting agency or organization not to accredit the National Defense University to award any new or existing degree.
